selector: native window.confirm() OK button (browser-chrome, no DOM selector)
label: OK (native confirm)
expected_behavior: Confirms the account-erasure warning. _gdprRequestErasure (dashboard-api.js:382) shows window.confirm(warning) then on OK imports firebase-functions and calls the 'requestAccountErasure' callable, toasting 'Confirmation email sent. Click the link within 24h...'. Deletion only completes via the emailed link, not here. NOTE: there is NO custom typed-confirmation modal for account deletion — the flagged typed-confirm modal does not exist; the gate is a single native confirm. In standalone/PWA mode window.confirm is patched (standalone-compat.js:154) to ALWAYS return true, silently bypassing this warning.
notes: Account-deletion confirm is a NATIVE browser confirm, not a typed-confirm modal. Cancel = native Cancel (aborts, returns from _gdprRequestErasure). QA cannot drive native confirm via DOM selectors; drive via dialog handler / accept|dismiss. Real risk: PWA confirm-patch bypass.

selector: native window.confirm() Cancel button (browser-chrome, no DOM selector)
label: Cancel (native confirm)
expected_behavior: Aborts account erasure. window.confirm returns false → _gdprRequestErasure returns immediately with no callable invoked and no toast. The account is NOT scheduled for deletion.
notes: Cancel-path of the destructive account-deletion flow. Must verify it truly aborts (and that the PWA confirm-patch does NOT auto-proceed, which it would).

label: OK
expected_behavior: Confirms the 'Delete N lead(s)? They will be moved to the trash.' nbdConfirm prompt raised by bulkDelete (crm-portal-bridge.js:250-253). On OK the promise resolves true → commitBulkLeadOp writeBatch sets {deleted:true, deletedAt:serverTimestamp()} on every selected lead (chunked at 500/batch), then loadLeads + clearBulkSelection + toggleBulkMode. Soft-delete (recoverable from trash), not a hard purge.
reveal_state: modal:nbdConfirm (after:click bulkDelete 🗑 Delete in bulk toolbar)
notes: bulkDelete uses the generic nbdConfirm modal (.sa-btn-ok/.sa-btn-cancel), NOT the dedicated #delConfirmOverlay (that one is single-lead only). Confirm button label is literally 'OK'.

label: Cancel
expected_behavior: Cancels the bulk-delete nbdConfirm. Promise resolves false → bulkDelete returns at crm-portal-bridge.js:251-253 with no batch write; selection is left intact. ESC key resolves false identically (standalone-compat.js:118-120).
reveal_state: modal:nbdConfirm (after:click bulkDelete 🗑 Delete in bulk toolbar)
notes: Cancel-path of bulkDelete. QA boundary control to drive-and-cancel.

label: Move to Deleted
expected_behavior: Confirm button of the DEDICATED single-lead delete modal (#delConfirmOverlay). confirmDeleteLead (crm-portal-bridge.js:506) hides the overlay, calls window._deleteLead(_pendingDeleteId) (soft-delete → moved to Deleted bin), toasts 'Lead moved to Deleted bin', refreshes trash badge. _pendingDeleteId is set by showDeleteConfirm (crm-portal-bridge.js:492) which is invoked from the card kebab delete and from map-overlay deletes (maps-overlays.js:347).
notes: This dedicated single-lead confirm modal (separate from nbdConfirm and from bulkDelete) was previously only represented by its trigger; its own confirm/cancel controls are this gap. Name shown in #delConfirmName (dashboard.html:8165).

label: Cancel
expected_behavior: Cancel button of the dedicated single-lead delete modal. cancelDeleteConfirm (crm-portal-bridge.js:500) clears _pendingDeleteId to null and removes the 'open' class from #delConfirmOverlay; no Firestore write occurs and the lead stays in the pipeline.
notes: Cancel-path of the single-lead soft-delete. Verify _pendingDeleteId is nulled so a subsequent stray confirmDeleteLead is a no-op.

label: OK
expected_behavior: Confirms the PERMANENT purge prompt 'Permanently delete "<name>"? This CANNOT be undone.' raised by permanentDeleteLead (crm-portal-bridge.js:584-586) when the per-card 'Remove' (.nbd-dc-perm) button is clicked in the deleted-leads drawer. On OK → window._permanentDeleteLead(id) which deleteDoc(doc(db,'leads',id)) for any id not prefixed 'd-' (dashboard-bootstrap.module.js:2601-2605) — a HARD irreversible delete — then toasts 'Permanently deleted', refreshes badge and re-renders the drawer.
reveal_state: modal:nbdConfirm (after:click Remove on a card inside the Deleted Leads drawer)
source_ref: crm-portal-bridge.js:557 (.nbd-dc-perm) → handler crm-portal-bridge.js:584-593; hard delete dashboard-bootstrap.module.js:2601
notes: Drawer permanent purge is the only HARD delete in the lead lifecycle (deleteDoc, not soft flag). Confirm uses generic nbdConfirm modal (.sa-btn-ok). Drawer body is dynamic; per-card Remove buttons are .dc-perm.nbd-dc-perm with data-id/data-name.

label: Cancel
expected_behavior: Cancels the permanent-purge prompt. Promise resolves false → permanentDeleteLead returns at crm-portal-bridge.js:586 with no deleteDoc; the lead remains in the trash/Deleted bin and is still restorable.
reveal_state: modal:nbdConfirm (after:click Remove on a card inside the Deleted Leads drawer)
notes: Cancel-path of the hard purge. Critical to verify it aborts in BOTH browser and PWA (PWA native-confirm fallback would auto-proceed, but this path uses nbdConfirm so the modal really renders).

label: OK
expected_behavior: Confirms 'Delete this photo? This cannot be undone.' raised by PhotoEngine._deletePhoto (photo-engine.js:1764-1772). On OK → deletePhotoFromFirebase(photoId), removes the photo-lightbox, toasts 'Photo deleted'. Irreversible (no soft-delete/restore for photos).
reveal_state: modal:nbdConfirm (after:click delete in the dashboard photo lightbox)
notes: Dashboard photo delete uses nbdConfirm. (customer.html deletePhoto uses NATIVE confirm — see row 011/012.)

label: Cancel
expected_behavior: Cancels the photo-delete prompt. Promise resolves false → _deletePhoto's if-block is skipped (photo-engine.js:1766); no Firebase delete, lightbox stays open, photo retained.
reveal_state: modal:nbdConfirm (after:click delete in the dashboard photo lightbox)

selector: native window.confirm() OK button (browser-chrome, no DOM selector)
label: OK (native confirm)
expected_behavior: Confirms 'Delete this photo? This cannot be undone.' raised by window.deletePhoto (customer.html:7039-7040). On OK → deleteDoc(doc(db,'photos',photoId)), removes from _allPhotos/_photoById, closes #photoActionPopup, recomputes stats + re-renders grid, reloads photos, toasts 'Photo deleted'. Trigger is the Delete button data-action=deletePhoto (customer.html:6957). NOTE: native confirm — patched to always-true in PWA (standalone-compat.js:154), so the gate is bypassed silently in standalone customer.html.
reveal_state: after:click Delete in the photo action popup
notes: customer.html photo delete uses NATIVE confirm (unlike dashboard's nbdConfirm). No DOM confirm/cancel selectors; drive via dialog accept. PWA bypass risk same as account-deletion.

selector: native window.confirm() Cancel button (browser-chrome, no DOM selector)
label: Cancel (native confirm)
expected_behavior: Aborts customer-record photo delete. window.confirm returns false → deletePhoto returns immediately (customer.html:7040 `if (!confirm(...)) return;`); no Firestore delete, popup and photo retained.
reveal_state: after:click Delete in the photo action popup
notes: Cancel-path of customer.html photo delete. Verify abort in browser; flag that PWA patch would NOT honor cancel.

route: #/settings (tab: Estimates → Product Library → product edit modal)
label: OK
expected_behavior: Confirms 'Delete this product?' raised by ProductLibrary.deleteFromModal (product-library.js:603-613). On OK → hardDeleteProduct(editingProduct.id) (permanent removal from the product library, distinct from archiveProductFromUI which only deactivates), closes the modal, toasts 'Product deleted', re-renders the list.
reveal_state: modal:nbdConfirm (after:click Delete inside the product edit/detail modal)
notes: Product hard-delete confirm (nbdConfirm). Note product-library.js:620 archiveProductFromUI uses native confirm for the non-destructive 'Archive this product?' — that is a soft action, out of this gap's scope.

route: #/settings (tab: Estimates → Product Library → product edit modal)
label: Cancel
expected_behavior: Cancels the product-delete prompt. Promise resolves false → the if-block in deleteFromModal is skipped (product-library.js:607); no hardDeleteProduct, modal stays open, product retained.
reveal_state: modal:nbdConfirm (after:click Delete inside the product edit/detail modal)

label: OK
expected_behavior: Confirm button of the generic nbdConfirm modal primitive (createModal type='confirm', standalone-compat.js:177 → 93-107). Resolves the returned Promise to true and removes the .sa-overlay. Also triggered by Enter key (standalone-compat.js:121-125). This is the shared OK control reused by bulkDelete, permanentDeleteLead, photo delete, product delete, and the customer.html stage-move confirm (customer.html:4537).
notes: Generic primitive row — the concrete destructive consumers are rowed individually above. Danger=none because the primitive itself is neutral; danger lives in each caller. Cancel counterpart is row 016.

label: Cancel
expected_behavior: Cancel button of the generic nbdConfirm modal primitive. Resolves the Promise to false and removes the .sa-overlay (standalone-compat.js:84-90). Also triggered by ESC key (standalone-compat.js:118-120). Every destructive caller treats false as abort/no-op.
notes: Generic primitive Cancel. Shared by all nbdConfirm-gated destructive flows.

label: Clear all
expected_behavior: FINDING — NO confirm dialog exists for clearAllNotifications. clearAll (notif-bell.js:608-613) immediately adds every non-dismissed notification id to the dismissed set, writes localStorage (DISMISS_KEY 'nbd_notif_dismissed_v1'), and re-renders. There is no confirm/cancel boundary control to drive. Dismissal is local-only (no Firestore), reversible via 'show dismissed' toggle, hence low risk.
notes: FINDING for the critic: clearAllNotifications has NO confirm dialog, so it contributes ZERO confirm/cancel rows. Rowed here only to document the absence — the trigger itself belongs to the bell/notifications gap, not this delete-confirm gap. No typed-confirmation input anywhere in scope.
